question: Why are my tomato leaves turning yellow?
description: I noticed yellow leaves on the lower part of my tomato plants. They are about 45 days old. Is this normal or should I be worried?

replies.text: This is likely nitrogen deficiency. Apply urea fertilizer at 50kg per acre. Also ensure proper watering.
I had the same problem last season. Use organic compost mixed with neem cake. It worked well for me.

question: Best time to harvest wheat in North India?
description: My wheat crop is 110 days old. When should I harvest for maximum yield? Weather has been dry lately.

replies.text: Wait until grains are hard and golden. Usually 115-120 days. Check moisture content - should be around 12-14%.

question: How to control weeds in paddy field without chemicals?
description: Looking for organic methods to control weeds. My field is 2 acres.

replies.text: Use cono weeder mechanical tool. Very effective and saves labor. I use it twice - at 20 and 35 days after transplanting.
Maintain 5-7cm water level continuously for first 30 days. This suppresses most weeds naturally.

question: Black spots on potato leaves - what is this disease?
description: I see black circular spots on leaves. Plants are 40 days old. Please help identify.

replies.text: Looks like Early Blight (Alternaria solani). Spray Mancozeb fungicide immediately. Remove infected leaves.

question: When to stop watering onions before harvest?
description: My onions are ready for harvest in 2 weeks. Should I stop irrigation now?

replies.text: Stop watering 10-15 days before harvest. This helps in better storage and curing of bulbs.

question: Best spacing for maize planting?
description: What is the recommended row-to-row and plant-to-plant spacing for hybrid maize?

replies.text: For hybrid maize: 60cm row-to-row and 20cm plant-to-plant. This gives optimal plant population of 83,000 plants per hectare.

question: How to identify pink bollworm infestation?
description: Want to know early signs of pink bollworm so I can take action quickly.

replies.text: Look for rosette flowers and holes in bolls. Use pheromone traps for early detection. If count crosses 8 per trap, spray immediately.

question: Red rot disease in sugarcane - treatment?
description: Some of my sugarcane plants show red rot symptoms. How to control spread?

replies.text: Remove and burn infected plants immediately. Treat remaining crop with Carbendazim. Use disease-resistant varieties in next season.

question: Chilli plants flowering but no fruit setting
description: My chilli plants have many flowers but they are falling off. No fruit development. What could be the reason?

replies.text: This is usually due to high temperature or water stress. Ensure regular irrigation. Spray NAA (Naphthalene Acetic Acid) 20ppm to improve fruit setting.

question: Should I remove tomato plant suckers?
description: I see side shoots growing between main stem and branches. Should these be removed?

replies.text: Yes, remove suckers for indeterminate varieties. This directs energy to fruit production. Keep 1-2 main stems for best results.
